questions.question:
Tavi the Pigeon places three different chillies on the ground.
He wants to eat them in every possible order.

Why does Biru the Monkey say backtracking is needed here?
options: Because only one order is required
Because all possible choices must be explored
Because the chillies must be sorted

questions.question:
Kaya the Lion starts forming one order of chillies.
After finishing it, Biru asks him to undo the last choice.

Why is undoing important in backtracking?

Biru the Monkey explains that backtracking depends on recursion.

What role does recursion play here?
options: It removes the need for choices
It handles breaking the problem into smaller parts

questions.question:
Kaya the Lion notices that a complete chilli order is formed.

What should happen at this point?

Biru the Monkey warns Kaya that forgetting to backtrack causes errors.

What kind of problem may occur?

Kaya the Lion compares recursion and backtracking.

What is the key difference?
options: Recursion breaks problems, backtracking undoes choices

questions.question:
Tavi the Pigeon asks when backtracking should be used.

Which situation fits best?
options: When only one solution is needed
When all possible solutions are required

questions.question:
Kaya the Lion writes backtracking logic.
One line is missing.
Which option completes it?
questions.code:
```
FOR each choice in options:
    ADD choice to current
    solve(remaining options)
    _________
```

questions.question:
Kaya the Lion reaches a point where no options remain.

What does this indicate?

Biru the Monkey reviews the logic.

Which line represents the backtracking step?
